comment.body:
From an Oil Expert Anas Abajji - What does President Trump want? (Below based on his statements)
🔰He wants to bring back the manufacturing of electronic chips and semiconductors to the United States instead of producing them in Asia
🔰 He wants to sell American agricultural products to India, but the Indians completely rejected that.
🔰 He wants Asian companies to sign long-term liquefied natural gas contracts with American companies and buy more American liquefied natural gas.
🔰He wants Asian countries to buy more American oil.

(Below based on the National Security Strategy published last November)
🔰Dominance over the world is achieved through artificial intelligence and an abundance of energy supplies, (but no AI without energy)
🔰Dominance over the world requires that energy be cheap for American AI companies and expensive for competing countries.

🔸The above requires control over all waterways. Panama Canal has been controlled, the Red Sea at both ends, and now the Strait of Hormuz to control the flow and costs of energy sources for competing countries. The remaining one is the Northern Sea Route near the pole, and that requires control over Greenland.

Has Trump achieved the above goals? He achieved all of them:
🪶Stopping the export of "helium" from the Gulf hit the Asian electronic chip and semiconductor industry. The United States is the world's largest producer of helium!

🪶Stopping the export of "fertilizers" to India hits the agricultural sector in India. But the matter is bigger: India's fertilizer industry relies on gas and liquid gases from the Gulf. These have been hit too. This will give Trump the opportunity to achieve the goal of forcing India to buy American agricultural products.

🪶Stopping exports of "methanol" from the Gulf hit a number of industries in China, South Korea, and Japan.

🪶It has now become clear to all Asian companies that relying on "oil and liquefied natural gas" from the Gulf is high-risk, and therefore diversification requires increasing dependence on American oil and liquefied natural gas. The United States is the world's largest producer of oil and liquefied natural gas.

🪶It is now clear that what happened in Venezuela and the stockpiling of Venezuelan oil in American ports was to compensate for the Iraqi oil that will not come to the United States due to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz! I was foolish when I thought that Trump and the global trading houses that bought the Venezuelan oil were having trouble marketing it. In addition, these companies are now selling it at double the purchase price!

🪶By providing insurance for ships and U.S. Navy escort through the strait, this means that Trump has indirectly occupied the strait and now controls the flow of energy to China and others, and the insurance and protection costs will raise the costs of oil and liquefied natural gas exported from the Gulf. In other words, the goal is to raise the cost.

In short, Trump achieved all his goals by using Iran as a pretext, which is why he expects the war to end with the Iranian regime remaining

comment.body: Throwing a dart here. Would love to learn prof! Tail put zomma is positive and increases into week end. ATM zomma is negative and increases in value towards week end.  Vomma tail positive and increase into week end. Vomma ATM negative and increases steeply (faster than Zomma) towards week end. Zomma is derivation of gamma by volatility. Tail put zomma is pos. So increase in volatility increases gamma, and this effect increases into week end. ATM the opposite, increase in vol surpresses gamma and the surpression effect reduces into week end. Vomma is vol of vol, i.e. change of Vega to vol. Tail put is positive vomma and increases. So if vol increase, Vega increases. This effect becomes stronger during week. ATM opposite, negative Vomma, so increase in vol. Surpresses Vega, but effect becomes less strong into weekend. And this effect reduces faster than gamma surpressions because steeper vomma surface than Zomma for ATM. Opposite effects all over if vol decreases. What does this mean? No I'm really blindly throwing darts. Because I'm not sure what's the logic to follow to model spot behaviour now with this info. Like what's the variable I think of in my head moving to see the reflexive effects back into it. Is it spot? Idon't understand your model or the Greeks sufficiently yet, but I'll just try. Don't ban me haha so assuming i didn't just commit multiple greek homicide but actually read it correctly, and you have us look at ATM and put tail surfaces, so it's about what happens when spot wants to go down. I just checked the dates so Monday is 9. So that means it's already in the 'steepness' or increasing/higher parts of vomma and zomma surfaces, not starting at the bottom of surface, so ATM the effects are already less strong than this weekend based on surface shape. If spot tries to go lower, gamma is surpressed, vega vurpressed, so dealers become shorter gamma relative,  so spot move down (or up) accelerates (relative to what it was before I guess, depending on gamma environment, since these are just rate of change based?, vega more long term effects if I'm not mistaken, but wouldn't know what to with it anyways). But the effect reduces, so spot accelerates down more early in the week than later if buyers don't step up. So if we falls down more likely early in the week. If spot gets to put tail area, opposite, gamma increases, dealers become longer gamma relative and so spot move down or up gets put on brakes, so stabilizing effect for spot. This gets stronger into week end. So the later we move down the stronger we are held up. Please help Alma:D

comment.body:
Alma, thank you for the exercise. Will also throw my thoughts in here to make my brain work harder (and hopefully learn a thing or two). 

Zomma is vol vs gamma. I expect customers being predominantly long gamma in current environment, MM short gamma. MM flow will exacerbate vol. Both surfaces Tail and ATM show steep positive curve which increases over the week heading into weekly opex. I think I remember you mentioned we need to first get beyond 12-13th before it can get better again, but do not know why this date is important (apart from opex). 

Vomma is vol vs vega.  Not sure I can see the curvature clearly, cannot turn picture, looks like tail curve is super steep beginning of the week and then falling sharply. ATM steeply positive. Overall higher vol will increase vomma. Effect decreasing over week, probably due to opex. 

Both greeks showing steep curvature in both tails and ATM likely means that there is vol bid across the curve, a sign of distressed market. Both effects are reinforcing each other. Zomma increases gamma creating higher vol, which feeds into higher vomma increasing the tail put hedges which will at a certain level turn into vol selling when customers realize the profits on their hedges. This may act as a circuit breaker in the tails. Next week will be high vol, but downside is absorbed at least at the beginning of the week.
